name: Keir Starmer
role: Prime Minister (Labour, 2024–present)
quote: Working people are the backbone of this country and we have let them down for too long. Under this government, they will always come first.

name: King Charles III
role: Monarch (acceded 8 September 2022)
quote: I am deeply aware of this great inheritance and of the duties and heavy responsibilities of sovereignty which have now passed to me.

name: Rishi Sunak
role: Prime Minister (Conservative, 2022–2024); Leader of the Opposition
quote: The United Kingdom is the greatest country on Earth. I will always do everything I can in my power to protect it.

name: Boris Johnson
role: Prime Minister (Conservative, 2019–2022); key Brexit architect
quote: There are no disasters, only opportunities. And, indeed, opportunities for fresh disasters.

name: Margaret Thatcher
role: Prime Minister (Conservative, 1979–1990); first female PM
quote: The lady's not for turning. You turn if you want to. The lady's not for turning!

name: Winston Churchill
role: Prime Minister (Conservative, 1940–1945 and 1951–1955); WWII leader
quote: We shall fight on the beaches, we shall fight on the landing grounds, we shall fight in the fields and in the streets, we shall never surrender.

name: Tony Blair
role: Prime Minister (Labour, 1997–2007); three election victories
quote: A day like today is not a day for sound bites, really. But I feel the hand of history on our shoulders in respect of this — I really do.

name: Clement Attlee
role: Prime Minister (Labour, 1945–1951); founded NHS, welfare state
quote: Democracy means government by discussion, but it is only effective if you can stop people talking.

name: Queen Elizabeth II
role: Monarch (1952–2022); longest-reigning British monarch
quote: I declare before you all that my whole life, whether it be long or short, shall be devoted to your service and the service of our great imperial family to which we all belong.

name: Gordon Brown
role: Prime Minister (Labour, 2007–2010); Chancellor 1997–2007; oversaw 2008 crisis response
quote: What we have done should give confidence and, I hope, inspiration that this global financial crisis can be resolved.

name: David Cameron
role: Prime Minister (Conservative, 2010–2016); called Brexit referendum
quote: I was the Prime Minister who put Britain's future to a vote. The people have spoken and it is right that I step down.

name: Nicola Sturgeon
role: First Minister of Scotland (SNP, 2014–2023); led IndyRef2 campaign
quote: Scotland's time will come. When the people of Scotland are ready, and they will be, we will have our referendum and we will win it.

name: Aneurin 'Nye' Bevan
role: Minister of Health (Labour, 1945–1951); founder of the NHS
quote: No society can legitimately call itself civilised if a sick person is denied medical aid because of lack of means.

name: Oliver Cromwell
role: Lord Protector of the Commonwealth (1653–1658); regicide
quote: You have sat too long here for any good you have been doing. Depart, I say, and let us have done with you. In the name of God, go!

name: William Gladstone
role: Prime Minister (Liberal, four times 1868–1894); Irish Home Rule champion
quote: The resources of civilisation against its enemies are not yet exhausted.

name: David Lloyd George
role: Prime Minister (Liberal, 1916–1922); WWI leader, welfare state pioneer
quote: Don't be afraid to take a big step if one is indicated. You can't cross a chasm in two small jumps.

name: Arthur Scargill
role: President, National Union of Mineworkers (1982–2002); led 1984–85 Miners' Strike
quote: The miners of this country are in the frontline of a class war — a war being waged by Thatcher on behalf of the ruling class.

name: William Wilberforce
role: Abolitionist MP (1780–1825); led campaign to abolish the slave trade
quote: You may choose to look the other way but you can never say again that you did not know.

name: Queen Elizabeth I
role: Monarch (1558–1603); Elizabethan golden age, defeated Armada
quote: I know I have the body of a weak and feeble woman, but I have the heart and stomach of a king, and of a king of England too.

name: John Maynard Keynes
role: Economist; architect of Keynesian economics and post-WWII Bretton Woods system
quote: The difficulty lies not so much in developing new ideas as in escaping from old ones.
